originalTitle: Thousands of anti-migrant protesters march in South Africa amid heavy police presence
title: Big News from Sunny South Africa!
content:
Hello, super kids all across our amazing continent, Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here to tell you about something happening in a beautiful country called South Africa. It's a place with big mountains, beautiful beaches, and lots of friendly people, just like where you live!

We're going to talk about a big walk and some friends from far away. It might sound a bit grown-up, but we'll make it super easy to understand. Let's find out what's been happening!

Friends from Far Away

Imagine you have a friend who lives next door. Now imagine you have a friend who lives in a different town, a bit far away. A *foreigner* is like a friend who comes from a different *country*! They might come to South Africa to visit, to work, or to find a new home for a while. They are just like us, but their first home was in another country.

Did you know? Africa has 54 different countries! Each country is special, with its own languages, songs, and yummy foods. We are all neighbors on this big continent!

A Big Walk and Talk

Sometimes, people want to share what they are thinking or feeling about something important. They might gather together and walk in a big group. This is called a 'march' or a 'protest'. It's like when you and your friends walk together to the playground, but this walk is to help everyone hear their message. They want to make sure people know what they believe.

In South Africa, many people went on a big walk. They wanted to talk about friends who came from other countries. They walked to show how they felt about people coming to live in their country.

Why Were People Walking?

Some of the people walking in South Africa felt very strongly about their home country. They felt that South Africa should be mostly for the people who were born there. They wanted the friends from other countries to go back to their own homes.

It's like when you have a special toy, and you really, really want to play with it yourself. These people felt that their country was their special place, and they wanted to keep it for themselves and their families who have always lived there.

Did you know? Everyone loves their home country! It's where we grow up, where we learn, and where we make our first friends. It's special to everyone.

A Special 'Finish By' Time

The groups of people who were walking had a strong message. They asked the friends from other countries to leave South Africa by a certain day. This special day is like a 'finish by' time. It's like when your teacher says, 'Please finish your drawing by lunchtime!' That's your deadline for the drawing.

So, these groups set a 'finish by' day for the friends from far away to go back to their own countries. It was a very important time for everyone involved.

Keeping Everyone Safe

When lots of people gather for a big walk or a special event, we need people to help keep everyone safe and calm. This is where police officers come in! Police officers are like the helpers who make sure rules are followed and that everyone stays out of harm's way. They wear uniforms and are there to watch over everyone.

Did you know? Police officers are sometimes called 'peace officers' because they help keep the peace and stop arguments. They want everyone to be safe and happy.

In South Africa, during this big walk, there were many police officers. They were there to make sure that the people walking were safe and that everything was peaceful. They wanted to make sure no one got hurt and that the walk was calm.

What Happened Next?

Because of these strong feelings and the 'finish by' time, many friends from far away countries who were living in South Africa decided to go back home. They packed their bags and went back to their own countries before the special day that had been set.

It was a big decision for them to make, to leave the homes they had made in South Africa and travel back to their original homes. They wanted to be safe and follow what was being asked of them.

Did you know? People travel for many reasons! Sometimes for fun holidays, sometimes to visit family, and sometimes to find a new place to live or work. Our world is full of amazing journeys!

So, that's the news from South Africa. It's about people expressing their feelings, and friends from far away making big choices about where they live. It reminds us that everyone has feelings, and sometimes big things happen when people want to share what's important to them. We should always try to be kind and understand each other, no matter where we come from. That's all for today, super kids! Stay curious and kind!.
summary: In South Africa, people marched to share their strong feelings about friends from other countries, and many of these friends decided to go back to their homes before a set deadline, with police watching to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how people in a country can have strong feelings about who lives there, and how friends from other countries might have to make big decisions about where they call home. It helps us understand that we should always try to be kind and understand each other, no matter where we are from.

title: Bigger than Rumble in the Jungle? DR Congo bid for World Cup history
content: DR Congo's run at the 2026 World Cup comes 52 years on from both their disastrous debut at the finals and one of sport's greatest occasions held in Kinshasa.

originalTitle: Flooding hits Ghana's capital killing 13 people - with another storm forecast
title: Hello Friends! Let's Talk About Big Rains and Staying Safe in Ghana!
content:
Hello everyone! It's your kid-friendly reporter from Africa, here to share some important news. Today, we're talking about something called 'flooding' in a wonderful country named Ghana. Ghana is in West Africa, a beautiful place with warm sunshine and friendly people. But sometimes, like in many parts of the world, Ghana gets a lot of rain.

What Happened in Ghana?

Recently, there was a really, really big rain in a city called Accra. Accra is the main city, or capital city, of Ghana. Imagine a city with lots of houses, roads, and busy markets. Well, the rain poured down so much that the water couldn't go away fast enough. This made the streets and homes fill up with water. When too much water covers the land, we call it flooding. The water came up high, making it difficult for people to move around. It was a very tricky and scary time for many families. The big rain made the water very strong and dangerous. Sadly, some people got very hurt, and we are thinking of their families right now. This is why it's super important to always stay safe when there is a big rain.

What is Flooding?

Flooding happens when there is so much water that it spills out of rivers, lakes, or even just off the land. It's like your bathtub overflowing, but much, much bigger! The water can come into houses and cover roads. It makes everything wet and messy. Did you know? Rain is super important for our world! It helps plants grow, fills up rivers, and gives us water to drink. But sometimes, when there is too much rain too quickly, it can cause floods. When floods happen, the water can be very powerful. It can carry things away, like small toys or even bigger things. That's why grown-ups always tell us to be careful around big water.

Staying Safe When It Rains

Because of the big rain and floods, grown-ups in Ghana are telling everyone to be very careful. They want people to move to a safe place or stay inside their homes. This is super good advice for all of us, no matter where we live in Africa or the world! If it's raining very hard, the best thing you can do is stay indoors. Play with your toys, read a book, or help a grown-up with something fun. Do not go outside to play in the rain or in the water that might be flooding. Did you know? Floodwater can hide things like sharp objects or deep holes! It can also have germs that can make you sick. So, it's never a good idea to play in floodwater.

What is High Ground?

The grown-ups are asking people to go to "high ground." What does that mean? Imagine a big hill or a place that is higher up than the area around it. That's high ground! Water usually flows downwards, so high ground is a safer place because the water is less likely to reach it. If you live in a place that might get flooded, your parents or guardians will know the safest place to go. Always listen to them! They know what is best to keep you safe and sound.

Another Storm is Coming!

The weather people, who are very clever at watching the sky, say that more rain might be coming to Ghana. This means another storm could happen. A storm is when the wind blows very hard and it rains a lot, sometimes with thunder and lightning. Because of this, it's even more important for everyone in Ghana to stay alert and follow the safety rules. Being prepared helps everyone stay calmer and safer. It's like knowing what to do before you play a game! Did you know? Clouds are made of tiny drops of water or ice! When these drops get too heavy, they fall as rain.

Who Helps Us?

When floods or big storms happen, many brave people come to help. There are firefighters, police officers, and other rescue workers. They work very hard to help people who are stuck or need to move to a safer place. Our neighbours and friends also help each other. In Africa, we are very good at taking care of our community! When someone needs help, we all try to lend a hand. It makes us stronger together.

What Can We Do?

Even as kids, you can help! How? By listening carefully to your parents and teachers. By staying calm. By not playing near dangerous water. And by being kind to your friends and family who might be worried or sad. We send our love and strength to all our friends in Ghana. We hope the sun shines brightly soon and the water goes away. Remember, safety comes first! Listen to your grown-ups, stay indoors if it's raining hard, and know that we are all thinking of you. Let's all remember these important lessons about staying safe during heavy rains. We are a big family here in Africa, and we always look out for each other. Be brave, be safe, and keep smiling!
summary: Heavy rains in Ghana caused flooding, prompting calls for people to move to high ground or stay indoors as another storm is expected, and everyone should prioritize safety.
whyMatters: This is important because floods can be very dangerous, make people sad, and damage homes, so knowing how to stay safe and listen to grown-ups during big rains is super important for everyone.
